name: Exceptionless Error Monitoring API
what_it_does: Open-source real-time error and log aggregation platform with REST API — originally built for .NET but supporting multiple languages, offering self-hosted deployment with full data control as an alternative to Sentry.
use_cases: Capturing and querying application errors from .NET and JavaScript applications
Self-hosted error tracking where application error data must stay on-premise
Querying error frequency and stack traces for automated incident triage agents
Integrating error data with project management tools via REST API
Building error alerting workflows triggered by new exception patterns
not_for: Teams needing extensive multi-language SDK support beyond .NET/JavaScript focus
Performance monitoring and APM (Exceptionless is error-focused, not APM)
Organizations needing enterprise commercial support from established vendor
best_when: A .NET-focused team needs open-source error tracking with self-hosting option and REST API, as an alternative to Sentry with full data ownership.
avoid_when: You need comprehensive multi-language support, APM features, or a well-established commercial vendor.

auth.notes: User token or API key from Exceptionless dashboard. Bearer token authentication. Organization and project-scoped access. Self-hosted instances manage their own auth. Token generated per user.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: open source self-hosted (unlimited); Cloud: free tier available
pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 open source — fully free to self-host. Elasticsearch required for self-hosted. Cloud managed service available. Smaller community than Sentry but solid .NET focus.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no limits; Cloud: plan-based limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covers events, projects, organizations, and stacks. OpenAPI spec available. Webhook support for new events and stacks. .NET SDK most mature — other language SDKs vary in quality.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Error event queries are idempotent reads. Event ingestion deduplicates by stack hash. Read operations always return consistent results.
known_agent_gotchas: Self-hosted requires Elasticsearch — significant infrastructure dependency
Smaller community than Sentry — fewer third-party integrations available
SDK quality varies by language — .NET is primary, others are community-maintained
Event retention limits apply on cloud tier — check data retention policy

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open source — fully auditable. TLS on cloud; configurable self-hosted. Token auth. Self-hosted provides full data control. No formal compliance certifications for cloud tier.
